description_long: This object is a segmented stone barrier composed of rectangular stone blocks arranged in a staggered, brick-like pattern. Two thick vertical stone pillars anchor the ends, while three horizontal stone slabs extend outward, each offset to create a stepped effect. The surface of the blocks is smooth with visible seams between each segment, suggesting a modular construction. The color is a uniform medium gray, typical of concrete or quarried stone, and the structure appears solid and heavy. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering. The overall design suggests it functions as a low urban barrier or decorative wall segment, likely used to demarcate spaces or prevent passage.
description: A medium-gray, segmented stone barrier with two vertical pillars and three staggered horizontal slabs, forming a stepped, modular wall. The surface is smooth and solid, with visible seams between the stone blocks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full stepped arrangement of three horizontal stone slabs anchored by two thick vertical pillars.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile and the depth of the stone blocks, with the staggered slabs extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same stepped pattern as the front, but with the slabs extending in the opposite direction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the offset of the horizontal slabs.
attribute_installation_method: mortared or set in concrete foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
